name: TDD-SUBAGENT-BACKLOG-4
title: When human-approval is bypassed, [rabbit] status print must continuously note it in a distinct non-critical color
description:
When the human-approval gate is bypassed (--no-human-approval mode), there is currently no persistent visual indicator. The user has no continuous reminder that the approval gate is off for the session.

Required behavior: while human-approval bypass is active, every [rabbit] hook status line should include a trailing note (e.g. '[approval-gate: off]') rendered in a distinct color that is clearly different from the scope-guard-off warning color. The distinction matters because:
- Scope-guard off is a security/integrity concern (critical color, e.g. red/orange)
- Approval-gate bypass is a workflow convenience choice (non-critical color, e.g. yellow or dim)

This gives the user ambient awareness without alarming them unnecessarily.
